rachel_yao.name: Rachel Yao
rachel_yao.summary:
Out of New Jersey, Rachel Yao is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-4 finish at Selection places Rachel in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Rachel is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

What makes Rachel especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 4 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-4 (U13 GD) • Junior Nationals top-4 (XD U13) • 1 ORC title.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Rachel boasts 21 three-game battles — proving Rachel is no stranger to the pressure of a decider, a career +538 point differential, a biggest win margin of 33 points.

Rachel has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 14 different players:
  • Ethan Qiu — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best: #2)
  • Justin Dong — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best: #2)
  • Melvin Cui — 6 events, 1 title together in U13/XD

Rachel is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 2 titles across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Rachel is still ascending.

Rachel has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Rachel has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 8 seeded entries across the career, Rachel consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 7 titles, Rachel is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

A true triple-threat, Rithik competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 8 singles titles, 6 doubles titles, 9 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.
